items.title: Ex-NST journalist Abdul Aziz Hassan dies at 71
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former New Straits Times journalist Abdul Aziz Hassan died this morning. A graduate in Mass Communications from Universiti Teknologi Mara (then ITM), Abdul Aziz was known for his contributions to journalism. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: New JPJ branch will operate seven days a week says Transport Minister (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Malaysians will soon be able to settle Road Transport Department (JPJ) matters seven days a week and until late at night at a new branch in Terminal Bersepadu Selatan (TBS), in what Transport Minister Anthony Loke described as a new model for public service delivery. Officially opening the new JPJ Bandar Tasik Selatan branch today, Loke said the facility would operate from 8.30am to 10pm daily, becoming the first JPJ branch in Kuala Lumpur to offer services every day of the week. Located on the third floor of TBS, the branch replaces JPJ’s Bandar Sri Permaisuri office, which had been operating since 2006.

items.title: Cathay Pacific sees growing Malaysia traffic as Hong Kong strengthens its hub role
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cathay Pacific is seeing continued growth in demand from Malaysia from both passenger travel and cargo services supported by growing connectivity through Hong Kong and continued exports of electronics and semiconductors. The airline's area head, Singapore and Malaysia, Nicolas Masse, said Cathay Pacific has added a fourth daily flight between Kuala Lumpur and Hong Kong from March 30, 2026, signalling the strong passenger travel demand. Cathay Pacific also operates dedicated freighter services from Penang to cater to the growing demand of cargo services from Malaysia.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends on softer note amid profit-taking
items.summary: Among the heavyweights, Maybank shed 22 sen to RM10.96, Public Bank eased 5.0 sen to RM4.90, Tenaga was flat at RM14.50, CIMB declined 2.0 sen to RM7.63, and IHH Healthcare was 9.0 sen easier at RM8.71. Of the most active stocks, Tanco edged down one sen to 13 sen, Zetrix AI rose half a sen to 78.5 sen, Dagang NeXchange was flat at 46.5 sen, GIIB slid 4.5 sen to 42 sen, and SKP Resources lost half a sen to 27.5 sen. Among the top gainers, UMS Integration rose 32 sen to RM9.02, SAM Engineering jumped 29 sen to RM5.19, Skyechip surged 27 sen to RM3.36, TMK Chemical soared 21 sen to RM2.75, and Kee Ming garnered 12 sen to RM1.47.

items.title: Traffic compounds for over 700 offences set to increase to RM500
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The maximum compound rate for more than 700 road traffic offences is expected to increase to RM500 if amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333) are passed by parliament. The amendments would also cover Section 59 on the inspection of vehicles, Section 66A involving registration number offences, and Section 72 concerning illegal parking and other parking-related offences. In addition, the proposed increase would affect offences under Section 76A relating to specific traffic enforcement matters, Section 79 involving failure to comply with traffic signs or directions, Section 102 concerning obstruction of enforcement officers, and Section 120 on general offences and penalties.

items.title: Coffee break: Starbucks Korea stores pause for training after 'Tank Day' fiasco
items.summary: SEOUL: Lights off. Coffee lovers across South Korea were left craving their fix on Monday as more than 2,000 Starbucks shops shut for staff training after a marketing fiasco. Starbucks Korea – the coffee giant's third-largest market after the United States and China – was embroiled in a major controversy last month over a promotion that evoked painful memories of a 1980 government crackdown on pro-democracy activists.

items.title: A decade of chaos: Britain prepares for seventh prime minister
items.summary: BRITISH Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he would quit on Monday, paving the way for the country to have its seventh leader in 10 years. Conservative Prime Minister David Cameron resigns and the party chooses Theresa May to succeed him. MAY 2019: BREXIT PARALYSIS, MAY RESIGNS, JOHNSON TAKES OVER May quits after failing to break a parliamentary deadlock over how Britain should leave the EU.

items.title: Police continue probe into defamation against Tok Batin in Jaslinda disappearance case
items.summary: IPOH: The investigation paper on a TikTok user accused of defaming and humiliating the Tok Batin of Kampung Lubuk Gaharu has been referred back to police by the Deputy Public Prosecutor's office for further investigation. "However, the investigation paper has been referred back to us to complete several outstanding matters in the investigation," he said at a press conference at the Perak police contingent headquarters here today. Following this, 50 Tok Batin and Orang Asli representatives from three districts have lodged police reports over alleged slander on social media against Lubuk Gaharu Tok Batin Mohd Asmmadi Abdullah .

items.title: Does Sedition Act apply to rebutting royals on politics, asks Pua
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former DAP MP Tony Pua has questioned whether those who respond to members of the royal family on political matters could be subjected to the Sedition Act. Pua asked in a Facebook post. asked Pua, a former three-term Petaling Jaya MP.

items.title: Lorry driver in FRU crash freed of concealing stolen cows charge
items.summary: TELUK INTAN: The lorry driver facing a charge of dangerous driving that caused the deaths of nine Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) members was acquitted and discharged by the Magistrate's Court here today of a charge of assisting in concealing two stolen cows last year. Magistrate Naidatul Athirah Azman made the ruling after finding that the prosecution had failed to prove a prima facie case against Rudi Zulkarnain Mat Radi, 46, at the end of the prosecution's case. Deputy public prosecutor Nur Darwis Aghniya Halim Azizi appeared for the prosecution, while lawyers Mohd Hafizuddin Khan Norkhan and Nur Intan Syakieraah Zakaria represented the accused.

items.title: After Starmer's announcement on resignation, how is his successor chosen?
items.summary: LONDON: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday he would resign, with a new leader to be in place by the time parliament returns in September, paving the way for Britain to have its seventh leader in 10 years. Any candidate wishing to replace Starmer would need to secure the support of 20 per cent of Labour After members of parliament. The winner then becomes prime minister.

items.title: Three Singaporeans escape unharmed in fatal Johor crash
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Three Singaporean passengers were unharmed after a collision involving a tour bus and a car claimed the life of a Malaysian motorist along Jalan Johor Baru-Mersing, here today. The fatal 7.45am crash occurred at Km57 of Jalan Johor Baru-Mersing involved the bus carrying three Singaporean men and a Honda City driven by the deceased 45-year-old man. Police detained the 45-year-old bus driver to facilitate investigations.

items.title: Bersama to contest 15 seats in Johor election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) said today it will contest 15 seats in the Johor state election next month, including seats currently held by Umno, DAP, PKR and Muda. In a joint statement, party leaders Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad said the seats were chosen based on voter trends, six months of survey data, local party strength, candidate suitability and campaign capacity. The 15 seats are Bukit Naning, Mahkota, Tiram, Puteri Wangsa, Johor Jaya, Permas, Larkin, Stulang, Perling, Kempas, Skudai, Kota Iskandar, Bukit Permai, Bukit Batu and Senai.

items.title: Finally open for business: Transport minister confirms LRT3 line starts June 29, PM to officiate launch on Sunday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The long-awaited Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) line linking Shah Alam and surrounding areas is set to begin operations next Monday June 29, Transport Minister Anthony Loke announced today. The line is expected to improve connectivity across the western corridor of the Klang Valley, serving key population centres including Petaling Jaya, Shah Alam and Klang while easing road congestion in one of the country’s busiest urban regions. The LRT3 line is expected to serve thousands of commuters daily and provide interchanges with existing rail networks, including the Kelana Jaya Line, Kajang Line and KTM services, further strengthening public transport integration across the Klang Valley.

items.title: Operators complain of high fees, Selangor studies ‘edu‑entertainment’ category for children’s playgrounds
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 22 — The Selangor state government is studying a proposal to reclassify indoor children’s playgrounds as ‘edu-entertainment’ to allow for a reduction in the entertainment duty imposed on such premises. State Tourism and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Ng Suee Lim said the study was initiated following complaints from operators who are required to pay entertainment duty of up to RM6,390 annually, even though the activities offered include educational elements. For this purpose, Ng said Selangor will hold engagement sessions with Penang, as the state has successfully introduced the ‘edu-entertainment’ classification without amending its enactment.

items.title: 5 hospitalised after mercury leak at courier facility in George Town
items.summary: Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said the spill was believed to have originated from a J&T Express parcel that broke during the sorting process. "The incident involved a mercury spill estimated at 500ml from a parcel reported to have broken while undergoing sorting operations. John said firefighters from the Jalan Perak fire and rescue station, assisted by the Bayan Baru fire and rescue station's Hazardous Materials (Hazmat) team, were deployed to the scene.

items.title: Hate narratives against Rohingya online may lead to violence, warns criminologist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The growing wave of hostility towards illegal immigrants online, particularly rhetoric directed at Rohingya refugees and undocumented migrants, risks normalising vigilantism and potentially violence if left unchecked. Senior Criminologist Datuk Dr P. Sundramoorthy warned that recent social media posts calling on members of the public to "round up" undocumented foreigners, including Rohingya migrants, should be viewed with serious concern as they reflect a dangerous process whereby hostility, prejudice and exclusion become normalised within public discourse. He told the New Straits Times that repeated exposure to messages portraying illegal immigrants or Rohingya communities as a threat to society could gradually lead some individuals to perceive discriminatory behaviour and even violence as justified responses.

items.title: Ex-supermarket supervisor jailed two years for CBT involving RM568,200 TnG reload value
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A former supermarket supervisor was in tears after being sentenced to two years' jail from today by the Sessions Court after pleading guilty to 14 charges of criminal breach of trust and money laundering involving RM568,200 worth of Touch 'n Go (TnG) eWallet reload pins intended for customers. She had initially faced two counts of CBT for transferring Aeon's TnG reload pins worth RM98,800 belonging to the supermarket in 2023 and 2024 into her own eWallet. "We do not take money laundering offences lightly.

items.title: Unloved and directionless, UK's Starmer quits after just two years
items.summary: KEIR Starmer was once hailed as the leader who would bring pragmatism and stability to Britain after years of political chaos. After guiding the Labour Party into power in 2024 with the biggest parliamentary majority in Britain's modern history, Starmer focused on what he believed was possible to achieve, rather than setting out a clear vision of a future Britain. He soon came to be seen by many voters and members of his party as lacking conviction and a clear direction, more than 20 party insiders said.

items.title: Selangor rolls out RM30m voucher scheme, 50,000 families to get RM100 monthly aid
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 22 — A total of 50,000 families in Selangor are eligible to apply for assistance through the ‘Kita Selangor Voucher’ initiative starting from noon tomorrow. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the assistance, amounting to RM100 per month for six months, involves a total allocation of RM30 million, with applications to be submitted entirely online through the official portal at www.baucarkitaselangor.com. He said the target group was identified based on data on applicants who have not received any existing assistance, such as the Bantuan Kehidupan Sejahtera Selangor (BINGKAS) programme, and in accordance with the databases of government agencies, including the Social Welfare Department and the Selangor Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU).

items.title: Govt rolls out three-tier plan to cushion food producers from global cost pressures
items.summary: Deputy Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Chan Foong Hin said the government’s response is divided into short-, medium- and long-term measures aimed at reducing production costs and strengthening food supply resilience. Chan said the government was also increasing financial support for paddy farmers, including raising paddy planting incentives for the 2026 season from RM160 to RM300 per hectare. For the medium term, Chan said the government is focusing on improving productivity across existing food production systems, including Permanent Food Production Parks, livestock and aquaculture zones, and Integrated Agricultural Development Areas.

items.title: Lorry driver in FRU crash cleared of stolen cow case but still faces trial over deadly accident
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, June 22 — The lorry driver facing a charge of dangerous driving that caused the deaths of nine Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) members was acquitted and discharged by the Magistrate’s Court here today of a charge of assisting in concealing two stolen cows last year. Magistrate Naidatul Athirah Azman made the ruling after finding that the prosecution had failed to prove a prima facie case against Rudi Zulkarnain Mat Radi, 46, at the end of the prosecution’s case. Deputy public prosecutor Nur Darwis Aghniya Halim Azizi appeared for the prosecution, while lawyers Mohd Hafizuddin Khan Norkhan and Nur Intan Syakieraah Zakaria represented the accused.

items.title: IJM Land expands Seremban 2 commercial portfolio on sustained township demand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IJM Land Bhd is expanding its commercial offerings across Seremban 2, supported by sustained demand within the township and surrounding residential growth. Nexus enters the market at a pivotal stage in Seremban 2's growth, as the township matures with a well-established residential base and expanding surrounding communities. "Commercial sustainability depends on real, everyday demand, and Nexus responds directly to how Seremban 2 lives, moves and grows," said Datuk Chai Kian Soon, chief operating officer of IJM Land.

items.title: Grab's introduces 'Group Ride' to promote carpooling, sustainable transport
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Grab Malaysia is responding to the growing national narrative of "Kongsi Perjalanan, Kongsi Tambang" (Share the Journey, Share the Fare) by introducing'Group Ride', a new feature designed to promote sustainable transport habits among Malaysians. AFFORDABLE AND EFFICIENT The Group Ride feature is built upon three core pillars: affordability, technological efficiency, and sustainability. • Host pays for all: The booking host covers the entire fare for the group.

items.title: Woman assaulted at Gelang Patah food stall, police probe viral clip
items.summary: JOHOR: Police have launched an investigation after a video showing a woman being assaulted by a man at a food stall in Gelang Patah went viral on social media. The 1-minute-30-second clip, which surfaced on June 20, shows a man striking a woman at a roadside stall. Iskandar Puteri district police chief M. Kumarasan said checks revealed that the woman is believed to be a worker at the stall operated by the male suspect.

items.title: Police arrest three for supplying communication equipment to online scam syndicates
items.summary: IPOH: Police have arrested three people, including two Chinese citizens, believed to be involved in supplying communication equipment to online scam syndicates. "Initial investigations revealed that the suspects were involved in supplying mobile phones and SIM cards to online scam syndicates operating in the country. Alwi said police also seized a large quantity of communication equipment and devices believed to have been used in the operation.

items.title: Two couples plead not guilty in Johor Bahru to charges of abusing and intimidating maids
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 22 — Two married couples claimed trial in the Magistrates’ Court here today to charges of causing injury to a female domestic helper at a home here last year. The accused, Muhamad Hirzihaziq Abulaes, 30, and his wife Nurfarah Ainna Hazhar, 34, together with Muhamad Firdaus Sha’aban, and his wife Nurafiqah Nabilah Hazhar, both 33, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read out before Magistrate Atifah Hazimah Wahab. In a separate Magistrate’s Court, Muhamad Firdaus Shaaban and Nurafiqah also faced two other charges of intentionally causing injury to two other maids, Yatina Yusnita and Siti Hajar, at a house in Jalan Delima 2/6, Taman Daya here between 2pm and 4pm on June 11.

items.title: Malaysia ramps up AI push with 60-month plan: Gobind
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is intensifying efforts to build a comprehensive artificial intelligence (AI) ecosystem over the next 60 months, with a focus on strengthening digital infrastructure, talent, governance and industry readiness to accelerate enterprise-wide AI adoption. "We are looking at how we can prepare our country for AI over the next 60 months. Many new policies have already been launched, including those related to energy, new industries and AI.

items.title: High Court increases ex-lawyer's jail term for RM392,000 CBT
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A former lawyer, convicted of criminal breach of trust (CBT) involving RM392,000 in land sale proceeds, has had his prison sentence enhanced from two to six years by the High Court. The High Court dismissed Subash Hassan Aboo Hanifa's appeal against his conviction on three charges under Section 409 of the Penal Code, while allowing the prosecution's cross-appeal for a heavier sentence. Judge Rofiah Mohamad upheld the conviction on all three charges and increased the jail term to six years for each charge, to run concurrently, while maintaining the RM30,000 fine imposed for each charge.

items.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation [WATCH]
items.summary: LONDON: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday after fewer than two years in office in a term characterised by policy U-turns and deep public unpopularity. Starmer said the process of picking a new Labour Party leader would be launched in July and he would remain as prime minister until his successor is chosen, to be in place in September. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Dennis Lim takes charge as Penang’s new police chief, vows peace, order and stronger community ties
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 22 — Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng, who was appointed new Penang police chief effective last Saturday, assumed his official duties at a handover ceremony today. He replaced Datuk Azizee Ismail at the handover ceremony at the Penang contingent police headquarters senior officers’ mess, which was witnessed by Bukit Aman Management Department director Datuk Seri Abdul Aziz Abdul Majid. As new Penang police chief, Lim stressed the commitment to ensure every police personnel carries out their duties professionally, with integrity and in compliance with the law and regulations.

items.title: APHM ready to support nationwide enlistment of private healthcare workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia (APHM) has expressed support for the Health Ministry's proposal to enlist private healthcare workers nationwide to strengthen public medical facilities. "Drawing from our experience in the private healthcare sector, APHM is keen to contribute to the conceptualisation of this effort and stands ready to collaborate with the Health Ministry in developing a practical, flexible and sustainable framework to support its implementation," it said. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad was reported as saying that the government will soon disclose more details on the possible nationwide enlistment of private healthcare workers to support public facilities.

items.title: Growing awareness sees Selangor children break silence, reporting abuse through Talian Kasih hotline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Growing awareness among children to report cases of abuse, bullying and neglect through the Talian Kasih 15999 hotline has contributed to the increase in reports received by the Selangor Social Welfare Department (JKM). Selangor JKM principal assistant director (Children Division) Roziah Osman said the rise in reports reflects the effectiveness of advocacy programmes in educating children about their rights and the channels available to seek help. In this regard, Roziah said Selangor JKM is targeting more than 500 tahfiz centres and religious boarding schools in the state through its Child Protection Advocacy programme, which will be expanded beginning July 27.

items.title: Indonesian maid in viral abuse case charged with injuring employer's toddler
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: An Indonesian domestic helper linked to a viral abuse case was charged in the Sessions Court here today with abusing her employer's one-year-old child last year. Yulia Arnida, 34, pleaded not guilty when the charge was read before judge Madihah Zainol. Defence counsel Jason Tan Ka Yan pleaded for lower bail, saying the accused has two school-going children, aged seven and eight, in Banda Aceh, Indonesia, as well as ailing parents.

items.title: Why we need a national cross-skilling framework
items.summary: LETTERS: Malaysia has invested heavily in improving graduate employability through curriculum reforms, industry collaborations, internships, and technical training. Yet one area that continues to receive relatively little attention is cross-skilling among university students. The benefits extend beyond individual students.

items.title: Nothing comes free: Study finds only 34pc of Malaysian content creators file income tax, many don’t think to declare gifts
items.summary: The report also said free products accounted for 46 per cent of a content creator’s overall income sources, higher than direct cash payments, which stood at 43 per cent. Only 34 per cent of the content creators polled have filed income tax and intend to continue doing so. Of the remaining 66 per cent, 14 per cent said they did not know that content creators must file income tax, while the majority said they were unsure if they qualified for income tax.

items.title: AI Living and the next evolution of residential property
items.summary: Property experts said this shift could represent a new normal in Malaysia's real estate development, as technology becomes more deeply integrated into how buildings operate and interact with occupants. Positioned as the world's first AI and robotics-driven residences, the AI Living concept could mark a new phase in Malaysia's residential market, where competitive advantage is defined not only by location, amenities or design, but by the level of intelligence integrated into the homes themselves. "From a property market perspective, AI Living is less a conventional residential launch than a test of whether developers can evolve into operators of technology-enabled living platforms," Tan said.

items.title: Five held over RM77,000 scratch-and-win scam targeting elderly
items.summary: Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the suspects, aged between 22 and 40, were detained in raids conducted in Melaka and Selangor on June 19. He said police had received two reports linked to the syndicate, involving two elderly women who were lured with promises of gold bars and lucky draw prizes. He said the suspects subsequently disappeared, while RM600 was withdrawn from the victim's bank account.

items.title: Cases of foreigners marrying locals for business licences on the rise in Selangor
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Cases involving foreign nationals allegedly entering into sham marriages with local women to secure business licences are becoming a serious concern in Selangor, the state government said. State Local Government and Tourism Committee chairman Datuk Ng Suee Lim said the practice was not new and was believed to have been taking place for more than a decade. He said some marriages were allegedly arranged to enable foreign nationals to operate businesses and remain in the country.

items.title: US and Iran conclude high-level talks in Switzerland
items.summary: BUERGENSTOCK, Switzerland: The first round of talks between high-ranking US and Iranian officials in Switzerland ended Monday, mediators said, after a tense opening marked by Teheran's announcement it had again closed the Strait of Hormuz and US President Donald Trump repeating his threats to resume attacks on Iran. A joint statement from mediating nations Qatar and Pakistan said the US and Iran agreed to a roadmap toward a final deal within 60 days. US Vice President JD Vance began talks with Iranian officials on Sunday under the terms of a memorandum of understanding reached last week to extend a tenuous ceasefire from April for at least another 60 days.

items.title: Malaysia has enough fertiliser stock for nine months, no immediate risk, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Deputy Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Chan Foong Hin today said Malaysia has about nine months’ worth of fertiliser stock and faces no immediate risk of disruption. He added that a proposal to establish a longer-term fertiliser buffer stock is still under review. “Based on engagement sessions with manufacturers and suppliers, Malaysia currently has sufficient fertiliser stock to last approximately nine months.

items.title: Two couples claim trial in viral maid abuse case [WATCH]
items.summary: All four accused pleaded not guilty to the charges before magistrates Atifah Hazimah Wahab, A. Shaarmini and Tan Chiew King. The accused are Muhamad Firdaus Sha'aban, 33, and his wife, Nurafiqah Nabila Hazhar, 33, as well as Muhamad Hirzihaziq Abulae, 30, and his wife, Nurfarah Ainna Hazhar, 34. In separate courtrooms before Shaarmini and Tan, Muhamad Firdaus and Nurafiqah Nabila faced two additional charges of causing hurt to two other domestic helpers.

items.title: Coroner sets Oct 23 as final date for Sabah schoolgirl Zara Qairina inquest hearing
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 22 — The Coroner’s Court here has set Oct 23 as the final date for the inquest hearing into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir, after noting that the proceedings had already gone on for 74 days. Coroner Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan said the court must keep track of the progress of the inquest, taking into account that July would mark the first anniversary of Zara Qairina’s death, while September would mark one year since the inquest began. Counsel Shahlan Jufri, who represents Zara Qairina’s mother, informed the court that the understanding was that the proceedings today would continue with Zara Qairina’s step-sister, while the first expert witness this month would only be available tomorrow.

items.title: Govt tables Cybercrime Bill in Parliament to replace outdated computer crimes law, cover AI offences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The Cybercrime Bill 2026, which seeks to repeal the Computer Crimes Act 1997 (Act 563), was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today. Ahmad Zahid said the Bill is intended, among other things, to repeal the Computer Crimes Act 1997 (Act 563) to ensure that Malaysia can meet its international obligations under the Budapest Convention (Council of Europe Convention on Cybercrime) and the United Nations Convention Against Cybercrime. “The Cybercrime Bill 2026 will provide regulatory and law enforcement powers relating to cybercrime and will be regulated by the National Cyber Security Agency (Nacsa) under the National Security Council (MKN), Prime Minister’s Department (JPM),” he said.

items.title: High Court reschedules ex-Youth and Sports official’s graft appeal to September amid defence application
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The appeal hearing of former Youth and Sports Ministry (KBS) finance division secretary Otman Arsahd against his 15-year jail sentence and RM284.2 million fine has been postponed to Sept 21. High Court judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin allowed an application by the defence to vacate the hearing dates scheduled for June 22, 23 and 25. The court fixed Sept 21, 22 and 23 for the appeal hearing and ordered the appellant to file written submissions by Aug 28.

items.title: Gobind: Malaysia to tighten laws on AI-generated child sexual exploitation material, deepfakes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the government is moving to strengthen existing legislation to address artificial intelligence (AI)-enabled offences, including deepfakes, identity impersonation and AI-generated child sexual exploitation material. He said Malaysia already has legal safeguards in place for certain crimes, particularly those involving children, but stressed that the legal framework must evolve to keep pace with emerging AI-related risks. For example, if we look at children, we already have existing criminal laws.

items.title: Leadership change at Yayasan Peneraju as Ibrahim Sani to leave CEO post July 1
items.summary: Minister of Economy and YP Board of Trustees chairman Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said Ibrahim’s leadership and contributions in steering the agency during an important phase of the organisation’s transformation, particularly in strengthening its role as a more strategic and value-oriented Bumiputera talent development institution. Akmal Nasrullah said the leadership transition would not affect the continuity of YP’s operations and strategic agenda, including its target of supporting the development of 100,000 Bumiputera talents by 2030 and developing 10,000 individuals with active economic participation and ownership, in line with the aspirations of the Bumiputera Economic Transformation Plan 2035 (PuTERA35). As part of this transformation, YP launched its Talent Bank in 2024 as a strategic platform for identifying and nurturing high-potential Bumiputera talents.

items.title: Govt may raise maximum compound for over 700 traffic offences to RM500
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The government is expected to seek an increase in the maximum compound rate for more than 700 road traffic offences to RM500 via proposed amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987, according to a report. The current maximum compound for various traffic summonses issued by the Road Transport Department (JPJ) and the police, including reckless driving, speeding, driving without a valid licence or road tax, and running a red light, is RM300. According to the report, the proposed increase would cover offences under several provisions of the Act, including those relating to motor vehicle licensing, vehicle licences, driving without a valid licence, third-party insurance, vehicle inspection, registration numbers, parking offences, failure to obey traffic directions and obstruction of enforcement officers.

items.title: Court in Ipoh sets July 1 for case involving woman accused of attacking Sultan Nazrin Shah
items.summary: IPOH, June 22 — The Magistrate’s Court here has set July 1 for case management involving a woman charged with using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, during the state-level National Day celebration last year. Magistrate Norizan Ridzuan fixed the date to record the plea of Nurhaswani Afni Mohamad Zorki, 41. The woman was charged with using criminal force against Sultan Nazrin by attempting to attack the ruler in front of the Ipoh Town Hall on Jalan Panglima Bukit Gantang Wahab at 8.20am on Aug 31, 2025.

items.title: Malaysia and Bangladesh vow to push Asean for lasting Rohingya solution, engage Myanmar authorities directly
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 22 — Malaysia and Bangladesh will strengthen cooperation through Asean mechanisms to find a lasting solution to the longstanding Rohingya refugee issue, including engagement with Myanmar’s authorities, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said both countries share a strong commitment to resolving the plight of Rohingya refugees currently residing in Bangladesh and Malaysia. He said Bangladesh looked forward to closer regional cooperation on several issues.

items.title: PM Anwar stresses migrant welfare as Tarique seeks more jobs, both demand fairer recruitment process
items.summary: Speaking at a joint press conference with Bangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman today, Anwar said while foreign workers remained critical to Malaysia’s economic growth, both countries must ensure their welfare and families are protected. We must protect the interests of these workers and their families,” said Anwar. Tarique said Bangladesh had requested Malaysia to consider recruiting more Bangladeshi workers and reopening its labour market as soon as possible.

items.title: Malaysia’s subsale home prices climb 4.8pc to RM545,059, Kuala Lumpur breaks RM1m mark
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Malaysia’s average subsale property price rose 4.8 per cent year-on-year to RM545,059 in the first quarter this year (Q1 2026), while the average price in Kuala Lumpur broke through the RM1 million barrier. IQI co-founder and group chief executive officer Kashif Ansari said the average price of a resale home in Malaysia climbed nearly five per cent over the past year, which reflects buyer confidence in the market. In Kuala Lumpur, the country’s largest urban resale market, buyers paid 15 per cent more on average for subsale homes in the first quarter of 2026, compared to a year earlier.

items.title: Azalina sets out seven reforms to separate powers and tighten Public Prosecutor accountability
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — A parliamentary committee has unveiled seven concrete proposals to reshape how Malaysia appoints its Public Prosecutor, including a fixed seven year term and annual reporting to Parliament. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the recommendations were part of a review of the Constitution (Amendment) (No. Azalina, who also chairs the special select committee on constitutional reform, said the Public Prosecutor should instead be appointed by the Yang di Pertuan Agong on the advice of the Judicial and Legal Service Commission.

items.title: Three elephants, RM53m allegations and a graft probe: MACC looks into Dara, Amoi and Kelat transfer from Taiping Zoo to Japan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has launched an investigation into allegations that payments linked to the transfer of Taiping Zoo elephants Dara, Amoi and Kelat to Japan did not reach the government, amid claims involving transactions worth about RM53 million. The commission said the investigation followed calls by wildlife advocacy group Hak Asasi Hidupan Liar Malaysia on June 18 for authorities to scrutinise the transfer. According to MACC, the group alleged that several payments related to the relocation had not reached the government as intended and linked several individuals to transactions worth about RM53 million.

items.title: Government studying new Bar Course as possible replacement for CLP, Kulasegaran tells Dewan Rakyat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The government plans to replace the Certificate in Legal Practice (CLP) with a new two-stage qualification route aimed at strengthening practical training for law graduates, Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said in Parliament today. He explained that the proposed new Bar Course (NBC) would involve a three-month online conversion programme on the Malaysian legal system, followed by a six-month postgraduate certificate focusing on legal practice skills, Free Malaysia Today (FMT) reported. Kulasegaran said the conversion course would be conducted online through a system to be developed by the Legal Profession Qualifying Board (LPQB).

items.title: Economy minister says inflation still moderate despite Hormuz disruption, but food and logistics costs under pressure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Malaysia’s inflation remains moderate despite global trade disruptions linked to tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, although cost pressures are rising across key sectors, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said today. However, Akmal said cost pressures were rising across several key sectors. Within the agriculture and food sectors, Akmal said 63 per cent of Malaysia’s fertiliser requirements depend on imports.

items.title: Malaysia conveys condolences as ex-Timor-Leste president Francisco Guterres, 71, dies in Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has conveyed Malaysia’s condolences to the family of former Timor-Leste president Francisco Guterres and the people of Timor-Leste over his passing. “I am saddened by the passing of the former President of Timor-Leste, Francisco Guterres. “Malaysia mourns his passing alongside the Timorese people.

items.title: Eight Bills receive royal assent from King, Speaker Johari announces in Dewan Rakyat
items.summary: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul announced this before the start of question time today in the house. He said those granted the royal assent were the Government Procurement Act 2025, Immigration (Amendment) Act 2025, Passports (Amendment) Act 2025, and the International Settlement Agreements Resulting from Mediation Act 2025. The Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link Act 2026, Capitation Grant Act 2026, Environmental Quality (Amendment) Act 2026, and Supplementary Supply (2025) Act 2026.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat Speaker confirms Hamzah’s return as Opposition Leader
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin reportedly resumed his duties as Opposition Leader in the Dewan Rakyat, effective June 18, Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul said today. “Effective from June 18, 2026, YB Larut has returned to the position of Leader of the Opposition,” he was quoted as saying in the Dewan Rakyat. Hamzah had previously served as Opposition Leader until May 20, before Kemaman MP Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar held the post from May 21 to June 10.

items.title: Illegal Alamesra colony torn down as DBKK demolishes 38 squatter homes, flags 112 more in mangrove zone
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 22 — The City Hall (DBKK), through its Enforcement Department, carried out an integrated operation to demolish illegal squatter structures behind Alamesra, Likas, on June 19 as part of ongoing efforts to address government land encroachment and unlawful settlements. The squatter homes encroaching mangrove reserves and riverbank zones. DBKK said 112 additional structures had been identified for further enforcement action and reiterated that it would not compromise on government land encroachment or illegal structures, adding that enforcement efforts would continue in line with the state government’s policy to eradicate squatter settlements and restore government land for lawful use.
